summary: The safety-limitations lane is acceptable for this lifecycle repair. The candidate does not alter safety evidence records or promote safety conclusions; it preserves that the broad senolytics coverage-repair candidate remains in needs_revision because safety extraction and event-level adverse-event limitations are still unresolved there.

findings.claim_or_issue: The repaired summary for senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-21 explicitly keeps safety-limitations gaps among the reasons that broad candidate remains in needs_revision.
findings.why_it_matters: Accepting this review lane should not be misread as clearing the underlying broad senolytics safety evidence for promotion.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the underlying senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-21 safety review finding open until a narrower safety extraction repair closes the event-level adverse-event limitations.

findings.claim_or_issue: The candidate-revision repair proposes only candidate_change record updates and does not create, update, accept, or release any safety outcome, result, finding, or synthesis record.
findings.why_it_matters: The safety review lane can be complete without independently rechecking source-level adverse-event extraction because no safety evidence record is being changed by this candidate.
findings.recommended_action: Limit this acceptance to the lifecycle repair scope and require separate supervisor review for any future safety evidence extraction candidate.

findings.claim_or_issue: The target repair candidate itself still needs the other required supervisor lanes before it could become promotion-ready.
findings.why_it_matters: A single accepting safety-limitations lane must not imply that source, extraction, taxonomy, or synthesis-boundary checks have been completed for this repair candidate.
findings.recommended_action: Leave candidate-revision-senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-21-repair unpromoted until all required active review lanes are complete and accepting.
